title: Distance
short: The separation between two objects, measured in metres (m).
full:
Distance is the separation between two points or objects. In force laws that fall off with separation it is decisive: Coulomb's law and gravitation are inverse-square, F ∝ 1/r², so doubling the distance quarters the force.

For point charges r is measured centre to centre. Always convert to metres before substituting.

The SI unit is the metre (m).
